page: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark results place the multi-core capabilities of this instance in the top tier. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score reaches 145450.5, and the PassMark CPU score is 132598.72, both ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 10740.63 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 255.02 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 2265.39, placing it in the middle 50%. Large-scale data processing is supported by a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 784.39 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 160.54 GB/sec.

The resource density of the D128ds_v7 offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory alongside substantial local storage capacity. This configuration is highly efficient for workloads that can leverage parallel processing and large local storage, such as database operations, which scored 34561.98 in PassMark database benchmarks, and Redis deployments, which achieved 204.83 million SET operations per second. However, the moderate single-core performance represents a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks. For machine learning workloads, the CPU supports AMX instructions, enabling prompt processing speeds of 44.7 tokens/second for a 70B LLM model, though text generation remains average at 4.78 tokens/second. Appropriate workloads include database hosting, large-scale web serving, and parallelized data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, 7559 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. The x86_64 architecture incorporates Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. In benchmarks, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, though single-core performance remains average. It is highly suited for parallelized workloads, database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and data processing tasks that benefit from large local storage and high multi-threaded compute capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a 128-vCPU general-purpose VM with 512 GB RAM, 7559 GB local storage, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs, massive local storage, and top-tier multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (64 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz.
512 GB of system memory and 7559 GB of local storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration for vector and matrix operations.
Well-suited for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and parallel data processing.
